## Task: Fix [high] UI/UX: Upload control is unreachable via keyboard (input is hidden via display:none)
## Step: review-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: high
File: src/app.ts:78-86
Title: Upload control is unreachable via keyboard (input is hidden via display:none)

Finding:
The file input is created with `uploadInput.hidden = true`, which applies the HTML `hidden` attribute → UA `display:none`. A `display:none` form control is removed from the tab order and cannot receive focus. The visible 'Upload' affordance is a wrapping `<label class="btn">`; labels themselves are not focusable/activatable via keyboard. Net effect: keyboard-only and screen-reader users have no way to focus or trigger the Upload control, while mouse users can (clicking the label forwards a click to the hidden input). This is a genuine WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure for the upload flow. The pattern was preserved through the refactor and lives in code touched by this changeset.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In `src/app.ts`, the upload input is built around the line `uploadInput.hidden = true;` inside `startApp` (the `const uploadInput = document.createElement('input'); uploadInput.type = 'file'; uploadInput.hidden = true; uploadInput.multiple = true;` block). Remove `uploadInput.hidden = true;` (do not set the `hidden` attribute). Instead, keep the input focusable but visually hidden: give it a class such as `visually-hidden` (e.g. `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden';`) and add `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files');`. Then in `src/app.css` add a `.visually-hidden { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }` rule (a standard sr-only pattern that preserves focusability). Because the `<label class="btn">` still wraps the input, Tab will now move focus onto the (visually-hidden) input inside the visible 'Upload' button, and Enter/Space will open the file picker. Verify that a visible focus ring appears around the Upload button when focused (add a `:focus-within` style on `.toolbar label.btn` if the default outline does not show). Do not change any other behavior.

Finding:
The file input is created with `uploadInput.hidden = true`, which applies the HTML `hidden` attribute → UA `display:none`. A `display:none` form control is removed from the tab order and cannot receive focus. The visible 'Upload' affordance is a wrapping `<label class="btn">`; labels themselves are not focusable/activatable via keyboard. Net effect: keyboard-only and screen-reader users have no way to focus or trigger the Upload control, while mouse users can (clicking the label forwards a click to the hidden input). This is a genuine WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure for the upload flow. The pattern was preserved through the refactor and lives in code touched by this changeset.
